chapter.label: Tale 131
chapter.title: The Life Of Abba Zachaios Of Holy Sion

paragraphs.text: Procopios the lawyer,* who came from Porphyredn, told us this about Abba Zachaios:
There was a deadly plague in Caesarea and I was very worried that my children might die. I did not know what to do. Should I send and bring them home? No man can {lee from the wrath of God.
Should I Jeave them there? They might die without me seeing them.

Not knowing what was best to be done, I said: ‘T will go to Abba Zachaios and whatever he says, that will I do’. So I went to Holy Sion (which is where he was always to be found), but I did not find him, I came into the inner court [of the Church of Saint Mary the

$otber of God and there I found him, standing in a corner of the court]. I told him about my sons. When he heard this, he turned towards the east and continued reaching up towards heaven for about two hours without saying a word. Than he turned towards me and said: ‘Take heart and do not be distraught: your children shall not die in the plague. In act, two days from now, the plague shall abate in Caesarea’, [and it came about as the elder foretold. As 1 said, this is what Procopios the lawyer told us.)
Abba Cyprian, whose surname was Cuculas and whose monastery is outside the gate of Caesarea told us this when we went to see him:

‘When that savage and horrendous plague ravaged this city, I shut myself in my cell and prayed the clemency of God to have mercy on us, to urn aside the wrath which threatened us. A voice came to me, saying: ‘Abba Zachaios has obtained this favour’.
